name: Spicy Scallops Stir-Fry
recipeIngredient: 2 Large Red bell peppers, cored, seeded and cut into 1-inch dice
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the scallops, 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp rice wine vinegar, 2 tbsp cornstarch, and 1/2 tsp sugar in a bowl.
2. In a separate bowl, combine the stock, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 3 tbsp rice wine vinegar, 2 tsp cornstarch, 2 tsp sugar, oyster sauce, sesame oil, and chili paste.
3. Blanch the snow peas for about a minute or two in a saucepan of lightly salted water. Refresh the peas under cold running water. Drain.
4. Heat 3 tbsp peanut oil in a wok over high heat until the oil starts to smoke. Lower the heat to medium-high.
5. Add the chili peppers and star anise. Cook until charred (1-2 minutes). Remove and discard the peppers and star anise.
6. Increase the heat to high. Add half of the scallops. Stir-fry until the scallops are firm and slightly browned (3-4 minutes).
7. Transfer the cooked scallops to a plate and repeat with the remaining scallops.
8. Add 1 tbsp oil, ginger, garlic, scallions, and bell pepper cubes to the wok. Stir-fry until the peppers start to soften (2-3 minutes).
9. Stir in the stock mixture and pour it into the wok. Add the scallops and snow peas. Stir-fry until the scallops are heated through and the sauce has thickened (1-2 minutes). Serve immediately.
